GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Move over marble, porcelain is set to be the next big interior trend", "Like stone and marble before it, Italian-made porcelain is about to enjoy a serious design moment.", "As far as architectural surfaces go, it's hard to look past the sleek, classic appeal of marble or natural stone. Certainly, it's a material that has endured, and you can see why it's found across countless bathrooms, kitchens and living spaces Australia wide. In fact, if you're looking to renovate or build, you'll see the tactile and luxurious material on almost every Pinterest board around — it's that popular. But despite its natural good looks, marble can be easily susceptible to damage in the home. Thanks to its porous nature, it's prone to stains and water marks, making it a high risk material for use in mess-attracting areas like kitchens and bathrooms. But what if we told you there was a material that looks just as good, but is even more durable than marble and natural stone? Enter porcelain, a hard-wearing, reliable product that offers the same beautiful aesthetic of marble, but isn't a total nightmare to clean or care for. Specifically, we're not talking about any old porcelain though — it's the quality of Atlas Plan you're after. Atlas Plan is an Italian-made porcelain that boasts a high-grade non-porous surface that's NSF-approved, as well as heat, chemical and stain resistant. Characterised as a ceramic product, Atlas Plan porcelain has been created using a highly technological process that fuses raw materials of approximately 30 per cent clay, 15 per cent quartz and 45 per cent feldspar together, with no glues or resins used. The result? A permanently hardened, ultra-compact surface that's UV stable and ideal for use in outdoor spaces such as building facades, paving and wall panelling, as well as high-traffic household areas like kitchen benchtops and splashbacks, sinks, tables, baths, bathroom vanities, shower walls and fireplaces. There's really no end to the different ways you can use Atlas Plan porcelain in your home. Atlas Plan porcelain isn't just robust — it's a highly versatile material that can be used across a plethora of applications. For example, the slab thickness can range from ultra-slim and modern versions to mitred options that create deeper edged profiles or waterfall features. In addition, Atlas Plan's versatile, large-format 1600mm x 3200mm porcelain panels make it easy for builders, architects or interior designers to work with. While Atlas Plan porcelain is familiar to the European market, it's a relatively new addition to the Australian design scene, and we predict it's about to become a whole lot more popular. Available in eight beautiful surfaces as part of a carefully curated Atlas Plan Australian Collection, the range includes several stunning marble and natural-stone looks. And don't miss two new editions: Super White and Absolute Black, which are coming soon. With sophistication, edge and durability at its heart, consider Atlas Plan porcelain the next frontier in interiors. You heard it here first. Atlas Plan Australian Collection is available in Australia exclusively through Smartstone."
